zh.lifeSummary: 當Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR於大約1205年出生在比利時海瑙特希馬時，她的父親Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR16歲，母親Agnès DU THOUR22歲。她於1226年1月與Jean DE SOISSONS II結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1240年享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR于大约1205年出生在比利时海瑙特希馬时，她的父亲Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR16岁，母亲Agnès DU THOUR22岁。她于1226年1月与Jean DE SOISSONS II结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1240年享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: CHIMAY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Amerindian (Mexico): from the root of Nahuatl (Aztec) chimalli ‘shield’.

nameMeanings.name: LE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: THOUR
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Erland, Eyvind, Nels.  Southeast Asian Chor, Tong, Chia, Chu, Kong, Seng, Tou, Chee, Ching, Chou, Chue, Eng, Thai, Moua, Neng, Pao, Yeng, Yer, Blong, Chong, Choua, Houa, Shoua, Soua.
Swedish and Danish: from a short form of any of various Scandinavian personal names containing the first element Thor (Old Norse Thórr), the name of the god of thunder in Scandinavian mythology.
English: from the Anglo-Scandinavian name Thōr, Thūr, probably short forms of Old Norse compound names in Thór-, Thúr- (see 1 above).

variants.name: Thorsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Thor, Niels, Lars, Anders, Arnt, Erik, Iver, Jarl, Knute, Magnar, Oystein, Viggo.
Danish and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Thor  .

variants.name: Thoren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Hilmer, Lennart.
Swedish (Thorén): ornamental name composed of the personal name Thor + the adjectival suffix -én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Thoresen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Birgit, Borge, Britt, Lars, Nils, Paal, Thoralf.
Danish and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Thor  .

variants.name: Thorsell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik.

Swedish: ornamental name from the personal name Thor + possessive -s- + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius. Alternatively, the element Thors may represent a placename formed with Tors-, as for example Torshälla.

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Thorell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lennart, Nils, Algot, Bjorn, Erik.
Swedish: ornamental name from the personal name Thor + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius. Compare Torell .

variants.name: Tharaldson
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian Tharaldsen: patronymic from the personal name Tharald, from Old Norse Thór(v)aldr, composed of the name of the god Thórr (see Thor ) + valdr ‘ruler’.

variants.name: Tellefsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Tellef, Aane, Ingeborg, Toralf, Torleif.
Norwegian: patronymic from Tellef, a form of the Old Norse personal name Thorleifr, composed of the name of the god Thórr (see Thor ) + leifr ‘heir, descendant’.

variants.name: Thore
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Middlesex): chiefly from the Middle English male personal name T(h)or(e), Thure, representing Old Norse Thórir or Thóri, or Anglo-Scandinavian Thórr, all short forms of Old Norse personal names containing the first element Thór-, Thúr- ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder in Scandinavian mythology. Occasionally perhaps also from the corresponding Middle English female personal name Thore (Old Norse Thóra). This surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.
Swedish: variant of   Thor  .
French (Thoré): nickname for a strong or violent individual, derived from Old French tor ‘bull’.

variants.name: Thorburn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain.
English: from a northern Middle English personal name T(h)orbern, T(h)urbern, Old Norse Thórbjörn, composed of the elements Thórr (see Thor ) + björn ‘bear, warrior’. Compare Durban .

nameMeanings.name: Marie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

en.lifeSummary: When Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR was born about 1205, in Chimay, Hainaut, Belgium, her father, Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR, was 16 and her mother, Agnès DU THOUR, was 22. She married Jean DE SOISSONS II in January 1226. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1240, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR est née environ 1205, à Chimay, Hainaut, Belgique, son père, Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Agnès DU THOUR, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Jean DE SOISSONS II en janvier 1226. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1240, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR um 1205 in Chimay, Hennegau, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR 16 und ihre Mutter Agnès DU THOUR 22. Sie hat im Januar 1226 Jean DE SOISSONS II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1240 im Alter von 36 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR è nata il circa 1205 nel Chimay, Hainaut, Belgio, sua padre, Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Agnès DU THOUR, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Jean DE SOISSONS II nel gennaio 1226. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1240, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.Chimay, Hainaut, Belgique: ベルギーヘイナトチメイ
ja.lifeSummary: Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOURが約1205年にベルギーヘイナトチメイで生まれた時，父Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOURは16歳，母Agnès DU THOURは22歳であった。1226年1月にJean DE SOISSONS IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1240年に36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR(이)가 대략 1205년 벨기에 하인오트 치메이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnès DU THOUR(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1226년 1월 Jean DE SOISSONS II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1240년 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR nasceu aproximadamente 1205, em Chimay, Hainaut, Bélgica, seu pai, Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Agnès DU THOUR, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Jean DE SOISSONS II em janeiro de 1226. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1240, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR родилась около 1205 г. в местности чимеи, ханаут, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnès DU THOUR, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean DE SOISSONS II январь 1226 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1240 г. в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marie DE CHIMAY LE THOUR nació aproximadamente 1205, en Chimay, Hainaut, Bélgica, su papá, Roger Ier DE CHIMAY DE THOUR, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Agnès DU THOUR, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Jean DE SOISSONS II en enero de 1226. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1240, a la edad de 36 años.
